title: Abort: `assert(!PyErr_Occurred())` in `unspecialize` (`specialize.c:378`)
description: specialize_load_global_lock_held is entered with a MemoryError ALREADY pending (set by a prior op under OOM; _SPECIALIZE_LOAD_GLOBAL runs before the lookup micro-op). The builtins/globals key lookup returns DKIX_ERROR because an exception is pending, so the code goto fail -> unspecialize(instr), whose `assert(!PyErr_Occurred())` aborts on debug builds. The de-opt backoff path does not tolerate a pre-existing pending exception. (NOT caused by the keys-version helper, which never allocates/raises.)
notes: Assertion `!PyErr_Occurred()' in unspecialize. CORRECTED root cause (gdb-verified, 2026-06): the MemoryError is PRE-EXISTING at specialize_load_global_lock_held entry (set by a prior op under the OOM sweep; _SPECIALIZE_LOAD_GLOBAL runs before the _LOAD_GLOBAL lookup). The builtins/globals _PyDictKeys_StringLookup returns DKIX_ERROR because an exception is already pending -> goto fail at the DKIX_ERROR branch (~specialize.c:1409), NOT the keys-version branch -> unspecialize asserts. The keys-version helper _PyDict_GetKeysVersionForCurrentState/get_next_dict_keys_version does NO allocation and never sets an exception (already exception-neutral, not even reached on the crashing run) -- the earlier 'helper sets a MemoryError' diagnosis was wrong. Real defect: the de-opt/backoff path runs unspecialize (assert !PyErr_Occurred()) while a pre-existing exception is pending. Debug-only assert (NDEBUG strips it). Exception-state-under-OOM family (cf OOM-0008/0010/0011/0015); distinct from OOM-0011 (specialize LoadAttr at specialize.c:364). Fix: don't enter/backoff specialization with a pending exception, or relax the assert to allow a pending MemoryError (drop the earlier 'make the version helper exception-neutral' fix -- it already is). Minimization DONE (2026-06-19): deterministic stdlib reduction in repro.py, shrinkray-reduced from the sys-fuzz vehicle, re-verified 20/20. Load-bearing: func(*args) (a specialized CALL, not func()) and a bare undefined global in the finally -- its LOAD_GLOBAL is the instruction specialized at the crash AND it keeps a pre-existing MemoryError pending into the specializer (it does NOT itself allocate). A defined name (raise RuntimeError) does not reproduce.
